elements.sent-2411-001: Abstract:~ We give characterizations of the transition semigroup and generator of a continuous-time Derrida--Retaux type process that generalizes the one introduced by Hu, Mallein and Pain (Commun.. Math.
elements.sent-2411-002: It is shown that the process arises naturally as the scaling limit of the discrete-time max-type recursive models introduced by Hu and Shi (J.. Stat.
elements.sent-2411-003: Keywords and phrases:~ Max-type recursive model, Derrida--Retaux model, transition semigroup, generator, martingale problem, weak convergence, Skorokhod space 2020 Mathematics Subject Classification:~ 60H20, 60J25, 60J76.
elements.sent-2411-005: For any function $f$ on $\mbb{R _+:= [0,\infty)$, write \beqnn T_af(x)= f\circ T_a (x)= f((x-a)_+), \quad a,x\ge 0.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Then, given a probability measure $\mu_0$ on $\mbb{R _+$, we can define a sequence of probability measures $(\mu_n: n\ge 0)$ recursively by \beqlb \mu_{n+1 = (\mu_n^{*2 )\circ T_1^{-1 , \quad n\ge 0, \eeqlb where $\mu_n^{*2 = \mu_n*\mu_n$ denotes the convolution.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-007: The sequence $(\mu_n: n\ge 0)$ is called a Derrida--Retaux model, or simply a DR model.
elements.sent-2411-008: By \eqref{DR0recur1mu it is easy to see that \beqnn \int_{\mbb{R _+ x \mu_{n+1 (\mathrm{d x) = \int_{\mbb{R _+ (x-1)_+ \mu_n^{*2 (\mathrm{d x) \le 2\int_{\mbb{R _+ x \mu_n(\mathrm{d x).}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: The DR model is referred to as pinned if $F_\infty> 0$, and as unpinned if $F_\infty= 0$.
elements.sent-2411-010: One main problem in this study is to determine for which initial distribution $\mu_0$ the model is pinned or unpinned.
elements.sent-2411-011: It is believed that for a large class of recursive models, including the DR model, there is a highly non-trivial phase transition.
elements.sent-2411-012: To discuss the phase transition from the pinned to the unpinned regime, it is convenient to specify the mass of $\mu_0$ at the origin.
elements.sent-2411-013: Consider the decomposition: \beqnn \mu_0(\d x)= p\delta_0(\d x) + (1-p)\vartheta(\d x), \quad x\ge 0, \eeqnn where $0\le p\le 1$ is a constant and $\vartheta$ is a fixed probability measure carried by $(0,\infty)$.
elements.sent-2411-014: Let $F_\infty(p)$ denote the associated free energy.
elements.sent-2411-015: Then $p\mapsto F_\infty(p)$ is a decreasing function on $[0,1]$.
elements.sent-2411-016: Write $p_c\in [0,1]$ for the critical parameter distinguishing the pinned and the unpinned regimes, that is, \beqnn p_c= \sup\{p\in [0,1]: F_\infty(p)> 0\} \eeqnn with the convention $\sup\emptyset= 0$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Derrida and Retaux \cite{DeR14 conjectured that, under the assumption $p_c>0$ and some integrability conditions on $\vartheta$, there exists some constant $C>0$ such that \beqlb F_\infty(p)= \exp\Big(-\frac{C+o(1) {\sqrt{p_c-p }\Big), \quad p\uparrow p_c.}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: Another basic question is the asymptotic behavior of the sustainability probability $\mu_n(0,\infty)$ as $n\to \infty$.
elements.sent-2411-019: When $p= p_c$ and $\vartheta$ is carried by $\{1,2,\cdots\}$ it is expected that \beqlb \mu_n(\{1,2,\cdots\})= \frac{4 {n^2 + o\Big(\frac{1 {n^2}\Big), \quad n\to \infty.}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: A continuous-time version of the DR model was introduced by Hu et al.~\cite{HMP20 , who showed the model is exactly solvable and belongs to the universality class mentioned above.}
elements.sent-2411-021: By definition, the model is a continuous-time flow of probability measures $(\mu_t: t\ge 0)$ on $\mbb{R _+$ solving the differential equation: \beqlb \partial_t\mu_t = \mu_t^{*2 - \mu_t + \partial_x\mu_t 1_{\{x> 0\} , \quad t\ge 0.}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: Following Hu et al.~\cite{HMP20 , we call $(\mu_t: t\ge 0)$ a continuous-time DR model, or simply a CDR model.}
elements.sent-2411-023: A differential equation similar to \eqref{Cdifeq0a was informally derived by Derrida and Retaux \cite{DeR14 as the scaling limit of the model defined by \eqref{DR0recur1mu , which has played the key role in the prediction \eqref{DR0conj .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-024: The CDR model $(\mu_t: t\ge 0)$ is exactly solvable when it is started with the initial distribution \beqnn \mu_0(\d x)= p\delta_0(\d x) + (1-p)\lambda\e^{-\lambda x}\d x, \quad x\ge 0, \eeqnn where $0\le p\le 1$ and $\lambda>0$.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this case, the free energy is defined by \beqnn F_\infty(p,\lambda)= \lim_{t\to \infty \,\e^{-t}\!\int_{\mbb{R _+ x \mu_t(\mathrm{d x).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-026: A discrete-time generalization of the DR model was introduced and studied by Hu and Shi \cite{HuS18 .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Let $0\le \alpha\le 1$ and let $q= \{q_1,q_2,\cdots\}$ be a fixed discrete probability distribution on $\{1,2,\cdots\}$.
elements.sent-2411-028: Given a Borel probability measure $\mu$ on $\mbb{R _+$, we define the measure $\mu^q$ by \beqlb \mu^q= \sum_{k=1 ^\infty q_k\mu^{*k , \eeqlb where $\mu^{*k}$ denotes the $k$-fold convolution.}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: The max-type model of Hu and Shi \cite{HuS18 can be defined by the recursive formula \beqlb \mu_{n+1 = [(1-\alpha)\mu_n + \alpha\mu_n*\mu_n^q]\circ T_1^{-1 , \quad n\ge 0.}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: When $\alpha= q_1= 1$, it reduces to the classical model \eqref{DR0recur1mu .}
elements.sent-2411-031: For the generalized DR model, Hu and Shi \cite{HuS18 showed a wide range for the exponent of the free energy in the nearly supercritical regime and Chen et al.~\cite{CHS22 established a weaker form of the conjecture \eqref{mu_n(0,infty)=4/n^2+o(.) .}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: A stronger result for the generalized DR-model with exponential-type marginal distributions was given by Li and Zhang \cite{LiZ24 .}
elements.sent-2411-033: In this work, we are interested in the scaling limits of the generalized DR model leading to continuities-time models like the one defined by \eqref{Cdifeq0a .}
elements.sent-2411-035: For $k\ge 1$ consider the rescaled measure $\gamma_n^{(k) (\d x)= \mu_n(k\d x)$.}

elements.sent-2411-037: This observation is confirmed by Theorem~\ref{thMDR-lim2a of this paper, where the convergence of the probabilities in a Wasserstein distance is proved.}

elements.sent-2411-040: For $t\ge 0$ and $f\in \b\mcr{C ^1(\mbb{R _+)$ let \beqlb A_tf(x)= a\int_{\mbb{R _+ [f(x+z)-f(x)]\mu_t^q(\d z) - f'(x)1_{\{x> 0\} , \quad x\ge 0.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: We shall see that $(\mu_t: t\ge 0)$ is a closed entrance law for $(P_{r,t : t\ge r\ge 0)$, that is, \beqnn \mu_t = \int_{\mbb{R _+}\mu_0(\d x)P_{0,t (x,\cdot), \quad t\ge 0.}}}
elements.sent-2411-042: We shall see that $(X_t: t\ge 0)$ is a generalized CDR process if and only if, for every $f\in \b\mcr{C ^1(\mbb{R _+)$, \beqlb f(X_t)= f(X_0) + \int_0^t A_sf(X_s)\d s + M_t(f), \quad t\ge 0, \eeqlb where $\{M_t(f): t\ge 0\}$ is a martingale.}}

elements.sent-2411-051: The remainder of the paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-052: The basic properties of the generalized DR models with continuous-times and discrete-times are discussed in Section~2, where the limit theorem for the rescaled dynamics $(\gamma_{\lfloor kt\rfloor ^{(k) : t\ge 0)$ is proved.}}
elements.sent-2411-053: In Section~3, we give characterizations of the transition semigroup and generator of the generalized CDR process.
elements.sent-2411-054: The martingale problem of the process is discussed in Section~4.
elements.sent-2411-055: The convergence of the rescaled process in the Skorokhod space is proved in Section~5.
